gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the Databricks runs ownership inversion and found one credential-hygiene issue: Databricks HTTP error bodies could echo bearer credentials and the CLI could persist that exception text in --output-json. The document-owned implementation now redacts the configured Databricks token and generic Bearer credentials before raising HTTP errors, regression tests cover direct API and CLI JSON-output paths, and GPT-5.5 re-review approved the branch.

issues.resolution: Added token and bearer-credential redaction before formatting Databricks HTTP errors, mirrored the private compatibility wrapper, and added direct plus CLI output-json regression coverage.
issues.summary: Databricks HTTP error bodies could persist echoed credentials in CLI JSON output.

scope: legacy module execution and helper monkeypatch migration hooks
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/databricks_runs.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/databricks_runs.py src/document_kv_cache/__init__.py tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
PYTHONPATH=src poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence
repository secret scan over README, package metadata, source, tests, Databricks helpers, PR evidence, and GitHub metadata
GPT-5.5 review and re-review after resolving the credential-redaction finding
what_changed: Moved Databricks run submit/get/status implementation into document_kv_cache.databricks_runs.
Routed document_kv_cache root Databricks run symbols to the document-owned module.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.databricks_runs into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.
Preserved legacy module execution, public and private helper monkeypatch behavior, pickle support, slotted config layout, and the previous legacy star-import surface.
Added Databricks HTTP error redaction for echoed configured tokens and Bearer credentials.
why: The generalized package should own Databricks run submission and status inspection because these helpers are shared by benchmark and release workflows. Keeping legacy compatibility makes this an incremental, behavior-preserving step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
